title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following an initial meeting where Dr. Kennedy vaguely identifies a facility as a tuberculosis sanatorium, Gwendolyn and Jules depart feeling the doctor is withholding information. Despite Jules's hesitation to continue the investigation, Gwendolyn insists on pursuing the truth regarding her father, Kelvin Hardy.

The following afternoon, Dr. Kennedy arranges a visit with Jules. While waiting, Gwendolyn speaks with Old Foster, who describes Dr. Kennedy as a good but dull doctor who ceased practicing fifteen years ago due to ill health. Foster also mentions that Dr. Kennedy's sister was considered "wild" and ran off with another man.

When Dr. Kennedy arrives, he reveals that the facility Kelvin Hardy entered was not a tuberculosis sanatorium, but a mental institution. He explains that Kelvin suffered a mental breakdown caused by extreme stress after his wife, Helen, left him for another man. Dr. Kennedy asserts that Kelvin’s belief that he had strangled Helen was merely a "bewitching delusion" resulting from pathological fantasies and unsettling dreams. 

Dr. Kennedy recounts a specific incident where Kelvin arrived at his examination room claiming, "I killed Helen." Gwendolyn notes that when they previously inspected the bedroom, there was no body and no sign of a struggle. Dr. Kennedy explains that he found a note from Helen in a living room wastebasket stating she was leaving Kelvin for the man she loved. Dr. Kennedy concludes that Kelvin saw the note and, in a state of mental instability, hallucinated the murder.
